answer:
\boxed{A}

The correct pair is **HC₂H₃O₂ (acetic acid)** and **C₂H₃O₂⁻ (acetate ion)**, as they differ by a single proton (H⁺), satisfying the Brønsted-Lowry definition of conjugate acid-base pairs. Options B, C, and D do not meet this criterion—SO₃²⁻/SO₂²⁻ are not proton transfers, H₃PO₄/H₃PO₃ involve oxidation state changes, and H₃O⁺/OH⁻ are not conjugates of the same species.
